name: The Goroutine Maze

enemies.name: Nil Panic
enemies.description: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference

enemies.name: Unhandled Error
enemies.description: if err != nil { /* TODO */ }. The TODO was never done.

enemies.name: Goroutine Leak
enemies.description: go func() — launched and forgotten. Leaking forever.
enemies.art:
go func
 go func
 go func
  LEAK!

enemies.name: Channel Deadlock
enemies.description: All goroutines are asleep. Deadlock detected.

enemies.name: Empty Interface
enemies.description: interface{}. Could be anything. Is probably the wrong thing.

enemies.name: Data Race
enemies.description: WARNING: DATA RACE. Read and write by different goroutines.

bosses.name: The Go Scheduler
bosses.description: M:N threading. It decides when you run. It decides when you stop.

bosses.name: The Generics Debate
bosses.description: 10 years of 'do we need generics?' made manifest. It has opinions.
bosses.art:
func F
 [T any]
 (x T)
   T  
 DEBATE

traps.name: Assignment to Nil Map
traps.description: Assignment to entry in nil map. Instant panic.

traps.name: Slice Bounds Out of Range
traps.description: runtime error: index out of range [5] with length 3

traps.name: Import Cycle
traps.description: import cycle not allowed: package a imports b imports a

empty: Channels carry whispered messages between empty rooms.
A defer stack unwinds silently as you pass.
go vet output scrolls across a clean terminal.
Goroutines sleep in neat queues along the walls.
The simplicity is deceptive. There's a lot happening concurrently.
rest: A sync.WaitGroup reaches zero. All goroutines done. Rest.
A properly closed channel. The signal is clear: rest.
A gofmt'd room. Everything aligned. Everything right.
treasure: A goroutine pool with perfect back-pressure control.
A context.WithCancel that was actually used correctly.
An error that was actually handled. With a real message.

floor_flavor: Goroutines scurry through the walls like mice...
The simplicity of Go is a thin veneer over deep concurrency.
Error handling: if err != nil fills every surface.
boss_clues: The goroutine count climbs into the thousands...
Channels begin to block. Something big is scheduling.
You hear the sound of a thousand 'if err != nil' checks.
